work.title: My Work
work.subtitle: A selection of projects I've built

findEat.description: Admin dashboard for FindEat (Canada) — enabling admins to manage clients, restaurants, and certifications.

mafal.description: Landing page for Mafal Dental clinic — built to increase online visibility and drive patient appointments.

uniAssist.description: Full-stack e-learning platform helping Algerian students succeed — courses, videos, exercises, and XP rewards.

swurvin.description: Mobile app connecting clients with tow truck drivers — live tracking, Stripe payments, and driver subscriptions.

findeat.name: FindEat
findeat.subtitle: Admin Dashboard
findeat.overview: Developed the comprehensive admin dashboard for FindEat, a Canadian food discovery and certification platform. The dashboard empowers admins to manage the entire platform ecosystem — from client onboarding to restaurant certifications — through an intuitive, real-time interface built with Nuxt.js and Firebase.

features.1: Client and restaurant management with full CRUD operations
features.2: Certification lifecycle tracking and approval workflows
features.3: Real-time data synchronization via Firebase Firestore
features.4: Role-based access control for admin and sub-admin users
features.5: Responsive design optimized for desktop operations

mafal.name: Mafal Dental
mafal.subtitle: Clinic Landing Page
mafal.overview: Designed and built a modern, conversion-focused landing page for Mafal Dental, a dental clinic looking to establish a strong online presence. The page showcases services, the team, and patient testimonials — optimized for SEO and performance to drive organic traffic and patient bookings.

features.1: Clean, professional design tailored to the medical industry
features.2: SEO-optimized structure and metadata for organic visibility
features.3: Responsive across all devices from mobile to desktop
features.4: Service showcase with smooth scroll animations
features.5: Contact form and appointment booking section

uniassist.name: UniAssist
uniassist.subtitle: E-Learning Platform
uniassist.overview: Built UniAssist from the ground up to help Algerian university students succeed academically. The platform provides structured courses, video lessons, exercises, a comments system, and a gamified XP rewards system to keep students engaged. The backend is a Django REST API, the web app is Nuxt.js, and the mobile app is React Native.

features.1: Structured course library with video lessons and exercises
features.2: Gamified XP and achievement system to boost engagement
features.3: Comment threads and community discussions per lesson
features.5: RESTful API built with Django and PostgreSQL

swurvin.name: Swurvin
swurvin.subtitle: Roadside Assistance App
swurvin.overview: Built Swurvin, a React Native mobile application that connects drivers in distress with nearby tow truck operators. The platform features real-time GPS tracking, in-app payments via Stripe, push notifications for accident alerts, and a subscription model for professional drivers.

features.1: Real-time GPS tracking for both drivers and operators
features.2: In-app Stripe payments with secure transaction handling
features.3: Push notifications for accident and emergency alerts
features.4: Driver subscription tiers with feature gating
features.5: Node.js REST API with PostgreSQL database

agents.title: AI Agents
agents.subtitle: Intelligent automation for the Brickcode platform

usage-agent.name: Usage Agent
usage-agent.subtitle: RAG Help Assistant
usage-agent.overview: A RAG-powered conversational agent that helps end-users navigate Brickcode — a modular multi-tenant SaaS platform. The agent answers product questions, guides users to the right screens and menus, and explains features without technical jargon. Built to reduce support burden by grounding every answer in the actual knowledge base: specs, UI flows, domain documentation.

steps.1: Receives a user question in natural language (French-first)
steps.2: Searches knowledge base with multi-step RAG (specs, domain, backend, frontend collections)
steps.3: Constructs a contextual, jargon-free answer with source grounding
steps.4: Returns a guided response pointing to the right screen or procedure

capabilities.1: Multi-collection RAG search across specs, domain, backend and frontend documents
capabilities.2: French-first responses with clear, non-technical language
capabilities.3: Guided navigation answers pointing to exact UI locations
capabilities.4: Context-aware follow-ups using LangChain agent memory
capabilities.5: Pluggable knowledge base via Chroma DB vector embeddings

spec-agent.name: Spec Agent
spec-agent.subtitle: Auto Spec Generator
spec-agent.overview: An agentic system that transforms a raw business need into two structured markdown specification files: a Domain model (entities, relationships, reused concepts) and a UserStories document (workflows, user scenarios). The agent searches the existing Brickcode knowledge base to reuse consistent terminology and patterns — eliminating manual spec writing and ensuring every new feature aligns with the established architecture.

steps.1: Takes a business need description as free-form text input
steps.2: Searches existing specs for similar feature structures
steps.3: Searches domain models for reusable entities and relationships
steps.4: Searches backend and frontend patterns for architectural conventions
steps.5: Generates Domain.md with entity definitions and UserStories.md with workflows
steps.6: Writes both markdown files to the target directory

capabilities.1: Generates Domain.md with entities, attributes and relationships
capabilities.2: Generates UserStories.md with structured scenarios and workflows
capabilities.3: Reuses existing terminology from the knowledge base for consistency
capabilities.4: Multi-step RAG retrieval across four document collections
capabilities.5: Write-to-disk capability via markdown file tool

sql-agent.name: SQL Agent
sql-agent.subtitle: Natural Language Data Queries
sql-agent.overview: A safety-first agent that allows non-technical team members to query live business data using plain English or French. The agent inspects the database schema before every query, writes read-only SQL, executes it, and returns the results as a clear natural language answer. Strict guardrails enforce that only SELECT and WITH statements are executed — no mutations ever reach the database.

steps.1: Receives a natural language question about business data
steps.2: Inspects the full database schema to understand available tables and columns
steps.3: Writes a targeted read-only SQL query (SELECT or WITH only)
steps.4: Executes the query against the database (SQLite or MariaDB)
steps.5: Translates the result rows into a clear natural language answer

capabilities.1: Schema-first workflow: always inspects structure before writing SQL
capabilities.2: Read-only enforcement: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP are blocked at system prompt level
capabilities.3: Supports both SQLite and MariaDB backends via SqlBackend interface
capabilities.4: Returns structured results with row count and column context
capabilities.5: Handles multi-table questions with JOIN and aggregation

orchestrator-agent.name: Orchestrator Agent
orchestrator-agent.subtitle: Intelligent Query Router
orchestrator-agent.overview: A high-level routing agent that serves as a single entry point for all platform questions. It classifies the incoming question and delegates to the appropriate specialist: the SQL Agent for data and statistics queries, and the Usage Agent for product navigation and procedural help. Users never need to know which agent to call — the Orchestrator decides and returns a unified answer in French.

steps.1: Receives any user question as input
steps.2: Classifies the question: data/statistics vs. product navigation/procedure
steps.3: Delegates to SQL Agent for counts, reports, or database content questions
steps.4: Delegates to Usage Agent for how-to, navigation, and feature questions
steps.5: Returns a unified, contextual answer in French

capabilities.1: Single entry point for both data and product questions
capabilities.2: Intelligent routing based on question semantics
capabilities.3: Wraps specialist agents as callable tools
capabilities.5: Extensible: new agents can be added as additional tools

brick-generator.name: Brick Generator
brick-generator.subtitle: Full-Stack Code Generation Agent
brick-generator.overview: The most complex agent in the suite. Given a Domain spec and UserStories file, it autonomously generates a complete production-ready Brickcode brick — a full-stack module consisting of NestJS backend files and Nuxt/Vue frontend files — following the destination brain's exact architectural conventions. Sub-agents handle backend and frontend generation separately, then all files are written, integrated into the monorepo, and validated automatically.

steps.1: Reads the Domain.md and UserStories.md spec files
steps.2: Reads the destination brain's structure and architectural conventions
steps.3: Searches inspiration brains for backend patterns (NestJS modules, DTOs, services)
steps.4: Sub-agent generates all backend files following found patterns
steps.5: Searches inspiration brains for frontend patterns (Nuxt pages, components, composables)
steps.6: Sub-agent generates all frontend files following found patterns
steps.7: Merges backend + frontend files and writes them to the destination brain
steps.8: Runs integration to register the new brick in parent configs
steps.9: Validates all required files exist and fixes any missing ones

capabilities.1: Generates complete NestJS modules: controllers, services, DTOs, entities, guards
capabilities.2: Generates complete Nuxt apps: pages, components, composables, stores, i18n
capabilities.3: Pattern-aware generation via RAG search on inspiration brains
capabilities.4: Automatic monorepo integration (registers brick in all parent configs)
capabilities.5: Self-validating: detects and fixes missing files after generation
capabilities.6: Sub-agent architecture separates concerns (backend vs. frontend generation)

brick-generator.tools: read_file · read_brain_context · search_brain_* · generate_backend_files · generate_frontend_files · write_files · integrate_brick · validate_integration
